questions.text: Which part of the plant anchors it to the soil?
questions.explanation: Roots anchor the plant firmly to the soil and absorb water and minerals from it.

questions.text: Plants with weak stems that spread on the ground are called creepers.
questions.explanation: Creepers are plants with weak stems that cannot stand upright and spread along the ground. Examples: pumpkin, watermelon.

questions.text: What is the function of stomata in leaves?
questions.correctAnswer: Stomata help in exchange of gases (oxygen and carbon dioxide) and transpiration.
questions.explanation: Stomata are tiny pores on leaf surfaces that allow gas exchange for photosynthesis and respiration, and also help in water loss through transpiration.

questions.text: The process by which plants lose water through leaves is called ________.
questions.explanation: Transpiration is the process of water loss from plants through stomata in the leaves. It helps in upward movement of water and minerals.
